messages.content:
翻译成中文：The U.S. Government estimates that there are 250,000 prisoners in Japanese Concentration Camps.
In Europe, the British Prime Minister, Attlee, declares that the atomic bomb has meant a brutal choice between world cooperation and global destruction.
In Yugoslavia, Parliament decrees that croplands over 75 acres will be taken over by the state and distributed among partisans and wounded soldiers.

美国政府估计日本集中营中有25万名囚犯。
英国首相艾德利宣称原子弹意味着一个残酷的选择，要么全球合作，要么全球毁灭。
南斯拉夫议会通过法令，决定将超过75英亩的耕地收归国有，并分发给游击队员和受伤士兵。

翻译成中文：During Operation Zipper, the Japanese officers stationed in Singapore have not readily accepted the Emperor's surrender order. Under such circumstances, General Seishiro Itagaki, commander of the defending troops orders that maximum resistance should be offered when enemy forces attempt to disembark. He then flies to Saigon to meet with Marshal Count Terauchi, who is in charge of all the forces of South and Southeast Asia in order to clarify the situation.
Upon his return to Singapore, General Itagaki explains to his forces that the order of the Emperor is authentic and must be obeyed. Singapore's daily press gives out the news, while Itagaki makes contact with Lord Mountbatten to say he is willing to surrender. In accordance with the Doctrine of Bushido, more than 300 officers commit suicide, after holding a farewell ceremony.
The Soviets occupy Port Arthur (Lüshunkou) at the southern tip of the Liaodong Peninsula. This action serves as revenge for the defeat that they had suffered at the hands of the Japanese Army, forty years earlier, in the Battle of the Yellow Sea during the course of the Russo-Japanese War between 1904 and 1908.

在行动“拉链”期间，驻守新加坡的日本军官并没有立即接受天皇的投降命令。在这种情况下，作为防守部队指挥官的板垣征四郎将军下令，当敌军试图登陆时必须进行最大限度的抵抗。然后，他飞往西贡与白求恩元帅会面，后者负责南亚和东南亚地区的所有军队，以澄清形势。
板垣将军返回新加坡后向部队解释，天皇的命令是真实的，并且必须服从。新加坡的日报发布了这一消息，同时板垣将军与蒙巴顿勋爵联系，表示愿意投降。根据武士道的教义，300多名军官在举行告别仪式后自杀。
苏联占领了辽东半岛南端的旅顺口。这个行动是对40年前，在第一次世界大战期间，他们在黄海战役中曾经败给日本军队的一次报复。

In England, in the town of Freckleton, to the south of Kirkham, a U.S. B-24 bomber flying out of the neighboring Warton air base, crashes into the Holy Trinity Church of England School killing 61 people, including 38 children.
In London, the two houses of the British Parliament ratify the United Nations Charter with the approval of all parties.

在英格兰，位于柯克汉姆南部的弗雷克尔顿镇，一架从邻近的沃顿空军基地起飞的美国B-24轰炸机坠毁在英国圣三一教堂学校，造成61人死亡，其中包括38名儿童。
在伦敦，英国议会两院经过各方批准，正式批准了《联合国宪章》。

In the Pacific, the small groups of Japanese soldiers who are still fighting on the island of New Guinea receive the news of the ceasefire, but the military leaders do not accept it and wait for confirmation from Marshall Hisaichi Terauchi, the Commander in Chief of the South Pacific region who has recently suffered a stroke.
The Japanese news agency in Tokyo transmits the order to all Japanese soldiers that they should evacuate all those areas where Allied troops have landed.
British and Chinese forces temporarily occupy Indochina to establish order, while the future of the region is decided.

在太平洋地区，仍在新几内亚岛上战斗的日本士兵小分队接到停火的消息，但军事领导人并未接受，而是等待南太平洋地区总司令寺内寿一元帅的确认，他最近罹患中风。
日本东京的新闻社向所有日本士兵传达了命令，要求他们撤离所有盟军登陆的地区。
英国和中国军队临时占领印度支那以确立秩序，同时决定了该地区的未来。

In London, the British Prime Minister, Clement Attlee, announces in the House of Commons that the U.S. Government has suddenly decided to end its ‘lend-lease’ policy without any previous consultation. For Attlee this decision leaves the country in "a very serious financial position."
During the debate, Winston Churchill, exclaims, "I cannot believe that a nation so large and generous could act in such an ill-mannered way towards its faithful ally."
In the United States, President Truman, during a press conference, answers questions about the cancellation of the activities related to the Lend-Lease Act. He downplays the issue and denies that such a cancellation is a terrible blow to the economies of Britain and other countries that benefited from such assistance. "The law was another weapon of war. It is now no longer necessary,” he says.

在伦敦，英国首相克莱门特·艾德礼在下议院宣布，美国政府突然决定结束“租借法案”政策，而之前没有进行任何磋商。对艾德礼来说，这个决定让英国陷入了“非常严重的财政困境”。

在辩论期间，温斯顿·丘吉尔惊呼：“我无法相信一个如此庞大和慷慨的国家会以如此无礼的方式对待它忠实的盟友。”

在美国，杜鲁门总统在一次新闻发布会上回答了有关取消与租借法案相关的活动的问题。他淡化了这个问题，并否认这样的取消对从这种援助中受益的英国和其他国家的经济造成了可怕的打击。“这项法律是战争的另一种武器。现在已经不再需要，"他说道。

In China, there is an increase in the clashes between the two sides in the civil war. Nationalist troops led by Chiang Kai-shek enter Shanghai and Nanking, the capital of the pre-war period. Mao's Communist forces announce that their troops are advancing towards those two cities, where workers have occupied the factories. In the south of the country they are moving towards Canton and in the north, they are approaching Tientsin (now Tianjin).
Allied aircraft drop ninety million leaflets over Prisoner of War camps in Malaysia, Burma and Siam (now Thailand) announcing the end of the war. At the same time, tons of medicines are dropped by parachute, especially Atabrine, to combat malaria.

在中国，内战双方之间的冲突加剧。蒋介石领导的国民党部队进入了上海和战前的首都南京。毛泽东的共产党部队宣布他们的部队正在朝着这两个城市前进，工人已经占领了工厂。南方他们正向广州逼近，北方他们正接近天津。

盟军飞机在马来西亚、缅甸和暹罗（现在的泰国）的战俘营上空投了九千万张传单，宣布战争结束。与此同时，大量药品，尤其是抗疟疾药物阿托品，通过降落伞投放，用于治疗疟疾。

In the Philippines, General Yamashita informs the American commanders that he has given the order to all Japanese soldiers to lay down their arms.
Radio Tokyo issues a report stating that a substantial number of people have committed suicide by harakiri, in front of the Imperial Palace.
Lieutenant Colonel Clay Tice Jr., commander of the 49th Fighter Squadron, makes an emergency landing and becomes the first American to set foot on Japanese soil following the victory.
In Europe, in the UK, according to government data, there are more than three hundred thousand German prisoners remaining and that the majority of them are working as agricultural laborers. In London, an international conference begins to reorganize internal transportation within Central Europe.

在菲律宾，山下奉文将军通知美国指挥官，他已下令所有日本士兵放下武器。
东京广播台发布报告称，相当数量的人在皇宫前自杀切腹。
乔治亚第49战斗中队指挥官克莱·泰斯中校紧急着陆，成为胜利后第一位踏上日本土地的美国人。
在欧洲，英国政府数据显示，仍有三十多万名德国战俘，其中大多数是农业劳工。在伦敦，一场国际会议开始重新组织中欧地区的内部交通。

A press release appears in a large number of newspapers around the world, issued by the British and American Governments, concerning their findings about the progress the Nazis had made on nuclear research, Intercontinental missiles, new submarines and stealth systems which were found to be in advanced stages of development.
To the amazement of the Allied experts, they had discovered that, at the end of the war in Europe, these German experts were working on a missile called the A-10 with a projected range of 4,828 kilometers (3,000 miles) capable of destroying everything within a large radius of its target.
In London, Arthur Harris, Commander in Chief of Bomber Command of the RAF, announces his resignation.

一份新闻稿在全球众多报纸上发表，由英美政府发布，关于他们对纳粹在核研究、洲际导弹、新型潜艇和隐身系统方面取得的进展的发现。他们发现纳粹在这些领域的研究已经处于先进阶段，这让盟军专家感到吃惊。

令盟军专家感到惊讶的是，他们在欧洲战争结束时发现，这些德国专家正在研制一种名为A-10的导弹，预计射程为4,828公里（3,000英里），能够摧毁目标周围大范围内的一切。

在伦敦，英国皇家空军轰炸机指挥部司令亚瑟·哈里斯宣布辞去职务。

The controversy over his performance has been going on for some time. Winston Churchill himself, in the final months of the conflict, warned that he was going too far with the indiscriminate bombing campaigns, as had happened for example, in the German city of Dresden.
Nevertheless, Harris, with his cold and determined character, justified his action concerning the missions that were carried out "under the orders of more important people than me." His way of seeing and understanding the situation was that a destructive dynamic was necessary and, in his opinion, it overrode the associated suffering of others.
In the Pacific, Soviet troops occupy the Kuril Islands to the north of Japan. Sixty-three thousand Japanese soldiers are taken prisoner.
The Japanese delegation, headed by General Seishiro Itagaki, and sent by Marshal Terauchi to negotiate the terms of surrender of the nearly seven hundred thousand Japanese soldiers that make up the Japanese forces in Southeast Asia, arrives in Rangoon (Burma).

对于亚瑟·哈里斯的表现，争议已经存在了一段时间。甚至温斯顿·丘吉尔本人在战争最后几个月都警告说他在进行无差别轰炸行动时走得太远了，就像在德国的德累斯顿市发生的情况一样。

然而，哈里斯凭借他冷静而坚定的性格，以“比我更重要的人的命令”来辩解他采取行动的合理性。他认为，需要一种破坏性的动力，他认为这种动力超越了他人的痛苦。

在太平洋地区，苏联军队占领了日本北部的千岛群岛，捕获了六万三千名日本士兵。

为了就东南亚作战的近70万日本士兵的投降条款进行谈判，由板垣征四郎将军领导的日本代表团被寺内寿一元帅派往仰光（缅甸）。

One of the biggest fleets ever to be gathered together lies off the coast of Japan, in Sagami Bay, the entrance to Tokyo. Three hundred and thirty-eight vessels make up the bulk of the occupation fleet. American, British, New Zealand, Australian, Canadian and Dutch ships are headed by a total of 23 aircraft carriers and await the arrival of General MacArthur.
The Third American fleet commanded by Admiral William Frederick Halsey, Jr. and protected by 1,200 aircraft lies off the coast of Japan. Twenty-one Japanese Government representatives are aboard the battleship "Missouri," planning the first phase of the occupation of Japanese territory by Allied forces.

有史以来规模最大的舰队之一聚集在日本海岸，位于相模湾，即通往东京的入口。共有338艘船组成了占领舰队的主力。美国、英国、新西兰、澳大利亚、加拿大和荷兰的船只由23艘航空母舰带头，并等待麦克阿瑟将军的到来。

由威廉·弗雷德里克·哈尔西海军上将指挥的第三舰队，由1200架飞机保护，停泊在日本海岸。21名日本政府代表乘坐战列舰“密苏里号”，筹划盟军占领日本领土的第一阶段行动。

General de Gaulle continues his visit to the United States where he receives a very warm welcome. During a ceremony, he lays a wreath on the grave of Roosevelt in Hyde Park, in Dutchess County, New York.
President Truman thinks that the situation in the Pacific is still dangerous and urges Congress to continue the recruitment of soldiers for two more years and to maintain a presence in the areas that have been taken in the region.
In eastern Burma, Allied troops make contact with the Japanese in the Valley of the Sittang River. The Japanese commanders await instructions to surrender from the delegation that is based in Rangoon.
戴高乐将军继续访问美国，他受到了非常热烈的欢迎。在一个仪式上，他在纽约州达奇斯县的海德公园向罗
